hints.Tetigit and exisse: 'Exisse' is an infinitive, not a finite verb — the finite verbs are 'Tetigit' and 'sensi'
hints.sensi and virtutem: 'Virtutem' is a noun — 'Tetigit' and 'sensi' are the finite verbs
hints.ego and aliquis: 'Ego' and 'aliquis' are pronouns — 'Tetigit' and 'sensi' are the verbs

hints.to cover: That would be 'tegere' — 'Tetigit' comes from 'tangere' (to touch)
hints.to weave: That would be 'texere' — 'Tetigit' comes from 'tangere' (to touch)
hints.to protect: That would be 'tueri' — 'Tetigit' comes from 'tangere' (to touch)

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'tangit' — 'Tetigit' with reduplicated te- is perfect
hints.perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'tetigerit' — 'Tetigit' is perfect indicative

hints.tego, tegere, texi, tectum: These are the parts of 'tegere' (to cover/protect)
hints.texo, texere, texui, textum: These are the parts of 'texere' (to weave)
hints.tueor, tueri, tuitus sum, —: These are the parts of 'tueri' (to watch/protect)

hints.to think: That would be 'cogitare/putare' — 'sensi' comes from 'sentire' (to feel/perceive)
hints.to know: That would be 'scire' — 'sensi' comes from 'sentire' (to feel)
hints.to hear: That would be 'audire' — 'sensi' comes from 'sentire' (to feel)

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'sentio' — 'sensi' is the perfect 1st person singular
hints.perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'senserim' — 'sensi' is perfect indicative

hints.scio, scire, scivi, scitum: These are the parts of 'scire' (to know)
hints.audio, audire, audivi, auditum: These are the parts of 'audire' (to hear)
hints.censeo, censere, censui, censum: These are the parts of 'censere' (to judge/assess)

steps.prompt: What is the grammatical function of 'exisse'?
steps.reveal: exisse = to have gone out (perf. inf. of 'exire')

hints.present infinitive: Present infinitive would be 'exire' — 'exisse' with -isse is the perfect infinitive
hints.complementary infinitive: A complementary infinitive completes verbs like 'volo' — 'exisse' is in indirect statement
hints.purpose infinitive: Purpose is expressed by 'ut' + subjunctive — 'exisse' is in indirect statement after 'sensi'

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'virtus' — 'virtutem' with -em is accusative

hints.dative: Dative would be 'alicui' — 'aliquis' is nominative subject

translation: Someone touched me, for I perceived power going out from me
